title: echium sabulicola subsp. rifeum
intro: Echium sabulicola subsp. rifeum, also known as Rife's spineless echium, is a subspecies of the Echium sabulicola plant, a member of the borage family (Boraginaceae). This succulent plant is native to the Canary Islands and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Echium sabulicola subsp. rifeum include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting immune function. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and ointments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal preparation methods.
